title: Medicham-Mega Battle Guide
summary: A [Fighting]/[Psychic] wallbreaker whose [pure-power] doubles an already strong attack stat for ridiculous effective damage.
strengths: [pure-power] roughly doubles an already strong 100 base attack
[Fighting]/[Psychic] STAB combo hits most of the metagame neutrally
weaknesses: 60/85/85 defensive line folds to any clean hit
Priority attackers and choice-scarf users outpace it easily

recommendedMoves.reason: Primary [Fighting] STAB that converts [pure-power] into instant KOs
Targets bulky [Water] and [Flying] switch-ins trying to wall the STABs

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Medicham-Mega is the textbook [pure-power] wallbreaker — a [Fighting]/[Psychic] attacker whose effective attack roughly doubles the moment it mega-evolves. Base 100 attack plus [pure-power] lands at roughly 400 effective attack, and base 100 Speed keeps it relevant in the Champions meta. The plan is simple: mega-evolve on entry, one-shot the first wall, and pivot out before a scarfer cleans it up.

## Strengths
After mega-evolution the effective attack easily rivals choice-band users without locking into a move. [high-jump-kick] by itself erases most [Steel], [Normal], and [Fighting] tanks that try to wall it, and [zen-headbutt] rounds out the STAB to crack [Poison] and [Fairy] pivots. [ice-punch] and [thunder-punch] handle [Dragon], [Flying], and [Water] switch-ins trying to [counter]-pick the STAB combo. Base 100 Speed outruns every common bulky threat in the tier without relying on paraphernalia.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 60/85/85 bulk is paper thin. Any priority user or choice-scarf pivot can [revenge]-kill Medicham-Mega cleanly, and the [Fighting]/[Psychic] chart leaves it helpless against [Ghost], [Fairy], and [Dark] walls that aren't already crumbling. [high-jump-kick] carries a hard punish on miss, so scout [protect] and [Ghost] switch-ins before committing. Turn one before mega-evolving is also dangerous because the original telepathy ability does nothing for the defensive profile.

## Recommended Moveset
[high-jump-kick] is the locked [Fighting] STAB because its damage after [pure-power] is the entire point of the build. [zen-headbutt] is the required second STAB that cracks [Poison] and [Fairy] cores trying to check the [Fighting] move. [ice-punch] is mandatory coverage for [Dragon], [Flying], and [Ground] pivots, and [thunder-punch] rounds out the set by punishing bulky [Water] and [Flying] switches. Some builds swap in [drain-punch] for longer games or [psycho-cut] for safer crit-friendly chip.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[medichamite] is the only legal item — mega evolution auto-locks [pure-power] and boosts both offensive stats. A [jolly] nature maximises Speed to win same-tier 100s mirrors, and the Tera choice is usually [Fighting] to juice [high-jump-kick] even further, or [Fairy] to flip [Dark] and [Dragon] coverage and [block] opposing priority abilities.

## Teammates & Synergy
Speed control partners such as [tailwind] users or [sticky-web] leads let Medicham-Mega outrun scarfers entirely. Pair it with a [Dark] or [Steel] partner to answer the Ghosts and Fairies that wall it, and run a [healing-wish] teammate to guarantee a second wave of pressure after the first cycle. [fake-out] leads are also great, because they buy the turn needed to mega-evolve safely.

## Play Patterns
Mega-evolve on the entry turn every time — there is no scenario where delaying [pure-power] is correct. Scout [Ghost] switches before committing [high-jump-kick], and line up Tera for the first predicted [Ghost] or [Dark] move. Once Medicham-Mega drops below 50% HP, prefer to pivot or sack it to a [healing-wish] partner rather than double-down on risky 50/50s.
